title: useRenderNodeRules: unstable build identity remounts nodes → input loses focus
description: Review finding #3 (Sol MED, Tanner P0). USER: BLOCKER for a form library. useRenderNodeRules memoizes on [build]; an inline (r)=>{...} builder changes identity every render, so renderNodeRules rebuilds the resolver, defeating NodeRenderer's memo bail → nodes remount → INPUT LOSES FOCUS, hook state resets. Fix options: (a) dev-mode warning when build identity changes (reuse the 'void tree' seam), (b) accept a rules OBJECT as an alternative stable form, (c) ref-latest + build-once with warn-on-change. Ship (a) at minimum; examples already use a module-scope builder so they're unaffected.
close_reason: Fixed: useRenderNodeRules captures the builder once via a ref (stable RenderNode for the component lifetime), so inline builders no longer remount fields — focus/value survive. Dev-only warning on build-identity change. Browser test locks it (useRenderNodeRules.stability.test.tsx).

title: Fix global mutable state in walk implementation
description:
**Location:** packages/core/src/parser.ts:388-399

**Problem:** Module-level mutable state (currentHandlers) is a major code smell that could cause bugs in concurrent/async scenarios and makes testing harder.

**Solution Options:**
1. Pass context through call stack (recommended)
2. Store handlers on node during parse
3. Use a Context class

**Priority:** Critical - This is the only serious code smell in the codebase

See: tmp/main/CODE_REVIEW.md for detailed solution examples

title: FormShapeOf ignores overrideWidgets → type/runtime widget desync
description: Review finding (Sol HIGH). FormShapeOf hardcodes NoOverrides, but useFormTree can re-present with overrideWidgets. Result: the type says widget 'choicegroup' while the runtime renders 'textarea' — a type/runtime desync invisible at the call site. Thread an Overrides seam into FormShapeOf, or explicitly scope/document that FormShapeOf reflects only the pre-override schema-derived widget.
notes: Doc guardrail landed pre-merge (PR #70): FormShapeOf (both front-ends) + useRenderNodeRules JSDoc + ADR 048 now state types reflect DEFAULT presentation and overrideWidgets can desync the typed Control. STILL OPEN: the real fix threads an Overrides map through the brand (WidgetAt<S,P,Overrides> seam already exists).
close_reason: Threaded overrideWidgets through the FormShape brand (ApplyWidgetOverrides in formShape.ts), so the tree's static type and runtime widget resolution stay in sync. useFormTree/useRenderNodeRules JSDoc updated. Added useFormTree.overrides.test.tsx to pin the re-narrowing. Residual 'wrong-tree footgun' (typing off the pre-override tree) noted in ADR 048 as a known limitation. Gate green.

title: FieldProps.value is compile-time-only but runtime passes undefined (contract mismatch)
description: Review finding (Sol HIGH). FormShape elevates value, so r.field('plan', ({value}) => ...) reads as strongly typed, but at runtime the handler receives undefined until form-state lands (ADR 041 §7). The type promises data the runtime does not yet provide. Either thread real values through the render props or narrow value's type to reflect its currently-unpopulated state so it can't be trusted prematurely.
close_reason: Fixed: FieldProps.value narrowed to 'schema type | undefined' so it can't be trusted before a form-state adapter lands (ADR 047 §7). Type test + example comments updated.

title: Complete TypedRuleRegistrar: typed array/control/allFields/where/default (typing cliff)
description: Review finding #2 (all three; Tanner P0, Pocock 'biggest gap', Sol MED). TypedRuleRegistrar only types field()/group(); array/control/allFields/where/default fall through to the untyped RuleRegistrar — a silent typing cliff. FormShape already carries arrays{}, so add ArrayProps<TS,P> and a typed control(kind). Bring the typed surface to parity with the primitive.
close_reason: TypedRuleRegistrar is now a complete registrar (type-alias over Omit<RuleRegistrar, field|group|array|control> + narrowed overrides): typed array(path)->ArrayProps<TS,P>, control(kind)->ControlProps<K> (Control narrowed to archetype, path/value stay wide), and allFields/allGroups/allArrays/where/default inherited un-narrowed. Kills the typing cliff. Added ArrayProps/ControlProps exports + type tests in useRenderNodeRules.test.tsx.

title: Type-level inference conformance oracle for sister front-ends
description: Review finding #4 (Sol HIGH; Tanner 'non-negotiable'). No test wires jsonSchemaToTree/zodToTree → FormShapeOf<typeof schema> → FieldProps end-to-end, so the front-end's 'as unknown as TypedTree' cast is unverified — the root-of-trust for the whole binding. Add a paired conformance oracle asserting the branded tree's resolved shape matches FieldProps narrowing (value types, widget→control, description presence/optionality) for both front-ends.
close_reason: Added end-to-end FormShape conformance oracle in packages/react (formShape.conformance.test.tsx): drives value/widget→control/description/path-set assertions off ShapeOf<typeof jsonSchemaToTree(schema)> and zodToTree, closing the unchecked 'as unknown as TypedTree<FormShapeOf<S>>' cast gap. Widget→control paired against runtime getField().parts.control.kind. Gate green.

title: Generalize React customize binding: resolved TypeSurface hung off the tree
description: Based on App17 findings, evaluate the elegant shape: front-end computes a resolved TypeSurface<S> = { fields: { [P]: { value; parts } }, groups: {...} } and brands its tree (TypedTree<TypeSurface>); React's useCustomize<TS>(tree, build) indexes into TS generically, importing no front-end. If it holds, react-jsonschema/react-zod evaporate. Open risk: compile-time perf of eagerly resolving every path's value+parts vs today's lazy per-handler ValueAt<S,P> — measure on a non-trivial schema. Also fold the front-end-agnostic composition (ControlAt/FieldPartsFor/GroupPartsFor) into a shared (Core?) layer so front-ends only provide SchemaAt/ValueAt/KindOf/DefaultWidgetAt/HasDescription.
close_reason: Front-ends brand the tree with a resolved FormShape; React binds off it via renderNodeRules/useRenderNodeRules. Recipes deleted. ADR 042. Gate green; perf measured linear.

title: App17 — react + Zod customize demo (ADR 008 forcing function)
description: Second implementation of the customize typed binding, over a Zod schema, mirroring App16. First pass: a customizeZod.ts recipe that is App16's recipe with imports swapped to input-zod — confirm it is near-identical and feel where Zod diverges (description/enum). This is the spike that decides whether per-front-end recipes/packages are warranted or whether the binding should generalize.
close_reason: App17 (React+Customize over Zod) + customizeZod.ts recipe added and registered in nav. Import-swap spike CONFIRMED: customizeZod.ts vs customizeJsonSchema.ts differ ONLY by (1) input-* import source and (2) defineSchema (Zod schemas are typed values, no const-literal capture) — all type machinery (Pretty/SlotsOf/FieldProps/GroupProps/TypedRegistrar/useCustomize) is byte-identical. Handlers in App17 mirror App16 field-for-field; enum arity narrows (plan→radio), and the description divergence is real+enforced (@ts-expect-error on parts.Description for a .describe()'d field holds because Zod descriptions are runtime-registry only). Full gate green. Verified via typecheck+gate (not a browser run). Strong evidence for bh7.3: generalize the binding, don't duplicate per front-end.

title: input-zod: thin type-level inference layer (infer.ts)
description: Mirror input-jsonschema/infer.ts for Zod v4 (introspect ZodObject shape / ZodArray element / wrappers via type-level inference on ZodType generics; ValueAt via z.infer). Thin is OK: cover object/string/number/enum/nested-object for the demo schema. Surface the known divergences: HasDescription likely returns false/unknown (descriptions live in z.globalRegistry, not the static type); DefaultWidgetAt enum arity is best-effort (Zod enum is a union). Export the same helper names as input-jsonschema so App17 can be a near-identical binding for comparison.
close_reason: Implemented input-zod/infer.ts (Zod v4 type-level inference), exported the full sister set (SchemaAt/KindOf/FieldPath(s)/GroupPaths/ArrayPaths/ValueAt/NodeAt/DefaultWidgetAt/WidgetAt/NoOverrides/ControlKindAt/ControlAt/HasDescription/FieldPartsFor/GroupPartsFor). Navigation via public class generics (ZodObject/ZodArray/ZodOptional...). Findings (proven by type probe): enum arity IS recoverable (union count → radio ≤5 / select >5); descriptions are runtime-registry only so HasDescription degrades to false. Paired type+runtime conformance test (infer.control.test.ts, 9 tests) green; full gate green.

title: Typed front-end inference contract + front-end-agnostic React customize binding
description: Generalize the customize typed-binding beyond JSON Schema. input-jsonschema has a type-level inference layer (infer.ts: FieldPaths/ValueAt/FieldPartsFor/ControlAt/...); input-zod has none (runtime zodToTree only). Build a second (Zod) implementation to force the seam (ADR 008), demo it (App17), discover the elegant shape (candidate: front-end hangs a resolved TypeSurface<S> off the tree; React binds off that phantom generically so react-jsonschema/react-zod evaporate), then lock a type-level conformance oracle for sister front-ends. Known divergences to resolve: Zod descriptions live in a runtime meta registry (HasDescription not statically computable); Zod enums are unions (radio-vs-select arity counting is fragile).
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Path-narrowed public types: FieldProps<S,P>/GroupProps<S,P> (path/kind/value/control/parts-presence); move NodeAt/ControlAt/PartsFor into input-jsonschema infer.ts (ADR 039 §4)
notes: ADR 039 refinement: control type routes through WidgetAt<S,P,Overrides={}> -> WidgetToControlKind. title: Arrangeable parts bag: Root/Label/Control/Description/Errors + {children}; promote Errors out of field Root; preserve control<->errors a11y ids across movable parts (ADR 039 §2)
notes: ADR 039 refinement: (1) rename whole-node Root -> top-level `Default` prop (mirrors engine {Default,Children}); it is exclusive with the parts and NOT a member of the parts bag. (2) reserve Root/Container for a future parts.container wrapper part. (3) every part slot gets an optional typed render prop <parts.X render={data=>...}/> with narrowed data (Label->{text,attrs,showRequired}, Description->{text}, Control->narrowed FieldControl, Errors->ValidationIssue[]).

close_reason: customize() selector registry with field/group/array/control/allFields/allGroups/allArrays/where/default + specificity cascade; handlers are mounted components (safe hooks)

title: Architect a source-neutral form rule runner
description:
Forms need value-driven behavior beyond validation: show/hide, enable/disable, required/optional, dynamic options, and set/clear/derive values. Build a source-neutral rule runner that evaluates conditions against current form values and applies deterministic effects without baking JSON Schema, Zod, React, or a specific form-state library into Core.

This is an architectural feature, not merely JSON Schema if/then/else support. It must serve both authoring modes: serializable tenant/DB rules (`rule_schema`) and code-authored static forms, while allowing source front-ends such as JSON Schema to translate native conditionals into the same normalized rule model later.

## Why now
ADR 011 explicitly identifies conditional fields and derived values as the remaining reason richer reactive form state may be needed. ADR 021 proved native FormData events are sufficient for live validation, but not whether they are sufficient for cross-field UI behavior. This runner will force the placement of reactivity, runtime field-state overlays, and validation interaction before JSON-Schema-specific conditionals or variant subforms hard-code the wrong seam.

## Scope to design and prove
- Conditions over paths and current values, including and/or/not and common equality, membership, emptiness, and presence checks.
- Declarative state overlays: visible/hidden, enabled/disabled, required/optional.
- Value/presentation effects: set value, clear value, and dynamic options. Treat these separately from state overlays because they mutate data and may trigger more rules.
- Nested object and array-instance paths, including the policy for rules inside repeated items.
- Deterministic ordering, conflicting effects, dependency tracking, cycle/fixed-point handling, and useful diagnostics.
- Evaluation timing and adapter integration (input/change/blur/submit), with a native FormData spike and an explicit decision about when a reactive form-state adapter is required.
- Submission semantics for hidden, unmounted, disabled, cleared, and preserved values.
- Validation semantics when dynamic requiredness conflicts with the source validator; browser constraint state and side-loaded validator truth must not silently diverge.
- Precedence among compiled schema facts, rule results, presentation resolvers, and consumer JSX overrides.
- Sync rules first; async conditions/effects and arbitrary external side effects are out of the first milestone unless a concrete scenario forces them.

Related work: jsonschema-form-avw (JSON Schema if/then/else) should translate into this capability rather than inventing a JSON-Schema-only runtime. jsonschema-form-c5t (variant subforms) is adjacent structural work but is not automatically the same abstraction.
design:
Start with a stubborn spike and ADR because this introduces a new capability seam and may pressure the stubborn Core boundary. Preserve Core as an immutable, stateless form-tree IR. Prefer a deep, framework-neutral evaluator with a small interface—conceptually current values + normalized rules + context in, per-path rule state and explicit value operations out. A form-state/framework adapter owns observation and application.

Do not begin with an enum of imperative UI commands. First separate: (1) condition evaluation, (2) declarative per-path state overlays, (3) value/presentation operations, and (4) adapter application. Resolve canonical state terms as visible/hidden, enabled/disabled, and required/optional; “show,” “hide,” “enable,” and “unrequire” are effects/transitions, not states.

A normalized rule model may be serializable so Mode 1 rule_schema can use it. Mode 2 may add typed/code-authored conveniences without making functions part of the portable representation. No source adapter may become the runtime rule engine.
acceptance_criteria:
- An ADR records the seam placement, normalized rule/result model, ownership of reactivity, and why Core remains unchanged or justifies any proposed Core change.
- A glossary update defines Rule, Condition, Effect, Rule state/overlay, and rule_schema without implementation details.
- A pure evaluator prototype covers nested paths, boolean condition composition, all three state axes, multiple rules targeting one path, deterministic conflict precedence, and cycle diagnostics for value-changing effects.
- A React/native-FormData tracer proves one realistic cross-field scenario without remounting unaffected uncontrolled inputs; findings explicitly decide whether native events suffice or a reactive form-state adapter is required.
- The golden scenario includes show/hide, enable/disable, required/optional, set/clear value, dynamic options, and nested/repeated fields.
- Hidden/unmounted/disabled value retention and submission behavior are explicit and tested.
- Dynamic requiredness has an explicit contract with browser attrs and side-loaded AJV/Zod validation; no silent disagreement is accepted.
- Rule evaluation is source-neutral. JSON Schema if/then/else can later compile/translate into it, but this feature does not make Core inspect source schemas.
- Async/external side effects are either deliberately excluded with an extension path or justified by a concrete second implementation.
- Follow-up implementation beads are split into independently reviewable PRs after the ADR/spike establishes the design.

title: Normalize validation errors terminology end to end
description: The pre-v1 validation protocol models only blocking failures, but mixes errors and issues across Core, adapters, and React. Normalize the owned public contract and implementation to errors while translating external Standard Schema/Zod issue fields only at adapter boundaries.
design: Human decision (2026-07-10): use the conventional form term errors everywhere in owned APIs because the protocol models only blocking failures and the 0.0.0 project has no external consumers. Do not add compatibility aliases. Preserve upstream field names only while reading external Standard Schema/Zod/AJV results, translating at adapter boundaries. This crosses Core and changes public interfaces, so record the decision in an ADR before proceeding.
acceptance_criteria: Core exports ValidationError and ValidationResult.errors; maintained validation adapters, React provider/hooks/store, tests, examples, and current docs use errors consistently; external protocol fields are translated at adapters; npm run gate passes.
notes: Implemented in PR #55: normalized owned validation vocabulary to ValidationError/errors across Core, maintained adapters, React, examples, and current docs; preserved upstream Standard Schema/Zod/AJV names at adapter seams; added ADR 037 and public contract coverage. Full npm run gate passes.
close_reason: Merged in PR #55 (merge commit 38b1dd9).

title: Fix Zod test node narrowing after schema-type threading
description: The latest main threads schema type S through AnyNode, but packages/input-zod/src/nodeTestUtils.ts still accepts only AnyNode<unknown>. This makes the root typecheck fail for Zod-origin array nodes. Mirror the generic narrowing signatures already used by input-jsonschema.
acceptance_criteria: All Zod node test helpers preserve S while narrowing; npm run gate passes on latest main.
notes: Implemented in PR #52; full gate green on latest main.
close_reason: Merged in PR #52 (merge commit 4af8303).

title: React: remove the useFormTree to ValidationProvider prop loopback
description: useFormTree owns errors, touched, and submitted, but every consumer must manually feed those values back into ValidationProvider. Deepen the native React binding so the correct validation state composes as one coherent capability while preserving consumer ownership of the form element, validation timing, display policy, summaries, and custom error UI.
design: Start by evaluating a spreadable validation object or another explicit boundary. Do not add a kitchen-sink Form component, hide revalidate timing, or auto-select a validator. Any bound component must have stable identity.
acceptance_criteria: The common validated form path no longer names and remaps three hook state values individually; ValidationSummary and custom children can share the same explicit boundary; touched and submitted defaults cannot be accidentally omitted; uncontrolled inputs never remount during validation updates; lower-level ValidationProvider and renderer interfaces remain usable.
notes: Implemented in PR #53 with the spreadable validation capability, ADR 036, stable identity/no-remount coverage, updated examples, and a green full gate.
close_reason: Merged in PR #53 (merge commit 48d6f70).

title: V1 DX finish: complete the schema-agnostic React experience
description: Turn the multi-perspective DX review into a coherent v1 experience without hiding meaningful IOC seams. This epic covers completion of the validated submit contract, validation composition, continuation-handle discoverability, async validation, product-first docs/examples, input support catalogs, package publishability, and branding.
design: Source: history/2026-07-10-multi-perspective-dx-review.md. Principle: keep meaningful choices explicit and remove accidental plumbing.
acceptance_criteria: All child issues are closed; the documented quick start works from an external consumer; compile, validation, timing, display, rendering, and form chrome remain independently composable; no source-specific React hook or kitchen-sink Form component is introduced.

title: Replace useSchemaForm with source-agnostic useFormTree
description: Remove the JSON-Schema-specific useSchemaForm hook. Add useFormTree(tree, options) as the sole React convenience hook so JSON Schema and Zod callers compile with their input package, then share identical bound rendering, submission, validation, touched state, and presentation.
acceptance_criteria: useFormTree is the only public convenience hook and is generic over tree origin; all useSchemaForm code/references are removed; JSON Schema and Zod trees work with matching validators; public-interface tests cover rendering/submission/validation/presentation; ADR and user docs describe compile-then-bind; npm run gate passes
close_reason: Replaced useSchemaForm with generic useFormTree(tree, options); JSON Schema and Zod share rendering/submission/validation/presentation behavior; React no longer peers on input-jsonschema; ADR 035, docs, examples, and tests updated; full gate green.

title: Zod as an input schema: zodToTree front-end (author a form from a Zod schema)
Add GitHub Actions CI: run the gate on PRs + require it before merge
description:
Today the gate (npm run gate = typecheck + lint + test) only runs locally, and branch protection does not require it — which is exactly how cm7 (#31) merged with a red React suite and left main broken until the #35 hotfix (twice-bitten). Add a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on pull_request (and push to main) and reproduces the gate, then enable branch protection on main to REQUIRE the check green before merge.

Scope:
- Workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml): checkout, setup-node with npm cache, npm ci, then run typecheck, lint (eslint), format:check (prettier), and test across all workspaces. Prefer running the same 'npm run gate' plus 'npm run format:check' so local and CI are identical. The React package uses Vitest browser mode with Playwright — install the Playwright browser (npx playwright install --with-deps chromium) before tests, or the browser suite will fail in CI.
- CRITICAL: do NOT pipe test/gate commands through tail/head/sed — that masks the real exit code (the bug that let cm7 merge red; see bd memory). Let each command's real exit code propagate so a red gate fails the job.
- Consider a matrix only if needed; a single Linux node job is fine to start. Cache npm and the Playwright browser to keep it fast/cheap (budget-conscious).
- Branch protection: require the CI status check to pass; optionally require branch up-to-date with main before merge. Document the setting (it's a GitHub repo setting, may need the owner to toggle).

Acceptance: opening a PR runs the workflow; a deliberately failing test blocks the merge button; a green gate allows it. This is the durable enforcement the ADR-009 verification-gated loop assumes.
close_reason: Added .github/workflows/ci.yml (gate on PRs + push to main); enabled main branch protection requiring the 'gate' check with enforce_admins; folded format:check into the gate. Red-path verified on #37 (failing test → merge BLOCKED). Merged #37.

title: cm7 regression: forced-multiselect submit stopped wrapping to array; React gate was red but masked by tail-piped exit code
close_reason: Fixed on main. Submit array-wrapping now keyed off the control archetype (facts.valueShape==='array' || select+multiple || choicegroup+multiple) in groupNode.ts (#35/#36), decoupled from widget names. Guarded by integration tests in parser.test.ts: 'ensures multiselect fields always return arrays (single value/multiple/oneOf)' + 'no selections omitted' — 9 multiselect submit tests green. The tail-piped exit-code masking that hid the original red is now structurally prevented by the CI gate job (#37, no tail/head/sed piping) + bd memory 'never-pipe-npm-run-gate-or-any-test'.

title: Tracer: present() stage + neutral FieldFacts + default widget rule (ADR 029)
description:
First vertical tracer for ADR 029, ONE PR, dual-period, commit-sequenced so the gate is green at every commit and main never carries the duplicated state.

Steps:
1. Add FieldFacts (path, label, description, required, primitive, valueShape:'scalar'|'array', format?, choices?, constraints, attrs, origin) to field nodes WITHOUT removing existing widget/parts.
2. Add Core present() + defaultPresentation (valueShape==='array' && choices -> multiselect; choices -> select; else input) + Core derivers for input/select/multiselect (extract from buildInputAttrs / select attrs in fieldNode.ts and createMultiselectFieldNode in arrayNode.ts). present() must preserve node identity for unchanged subtrees (structural sharing) so the React NodeRenderer memo-bail holds.
3. Wire useSchemaForm -> present().
4. Flip conformance to compare the post-present() tree.
5. Delete the parser's widget/parts logic; migrate/delete redundant parser unit tests into integration tests.

Golden integration test (product-owner style, assert DOM + submit, not internals): a schema with no multiselect hint + resolvePresentation forcing multiselect -> assert <select multiple> in the DOM and submit yields string[].

First tracer reuses the existing select dispatch (multiselect already renders via <select multiple>) so it does NOT include the field.control engine/adapter rewrite.

Deferred to later beads: bindRenders/extend custom-widget DX; unified field.control slot; origin rename (schema -> origin {source,schema:TOrigin}).
close_reason: Landed in PR #25 (rebase-merged to main: 70fbdba..7b09e0f)

title: Validator contract: purity invariant + transformed-data return (ADR 025)
description: Implement ADR 025. (1) Add optional `data?: unknown` to ValidationResult in packages/core/src/validation.ts. (2) AJV adapter: clone input before validate so coerceTypes never mutates the caller; return the coerced clone as result.data. (3) Zod adapter: return safeParse result.data on success. (4) validation-contract suite: add a purity assertion (input deep-equal before/after validate) and a transformed-data assertion (coerced '18'->18 reflected in result.data, input unchanged). (5) Delete the defensive JSON clone from the RHF recipe (App_12) now that the contract guarantees purity. Gate must stay green.
close_reason: Implemented ADR 025 on main (71a5918): ValidationResult<T> + data?, Validator purity invariant, AJV clones+returns coerced data (InferData<S>), Zod returns parsed output (TypeOf<T>), contract suite purity+no-aliasing assertions, per-adapter coercion tests, RHF recipe clone removed. Gate green.

title: Unify $ref resolution to walk allOf (single pass) + dedup JSON Pointer helpers
description: PR #8's mergeAllOf resolves $ref entries inside allOf only one level and duplicates decodeJsonPointerSegment/resolveJsonPointer from resolveRefs.ts. Teach resolveLocalRefs to walk allOf (or run a second resolution pass after merge) so chained $defs graphs inside allOf resolve, and extract shared JSON Pointer helpers. Add tests for items-level allOf and nested $ref/$defs graphs. Raised in PR #8 review.
notes: RHF spike added setNested/getNested dot-path helpers in the recipe (App_12) — more duplicated path manipulation alongside resolveRefs' JSON Pointer helpers, AJV's pointerToPath/join, and the engine's dense-array repathing. Reinforces consolidating into one shared core path-utils module. Pure get/set-by-dot-path is the reusable primitive; lift it here when a second non-recipe consumer appears (ADR 008).
close_reason: Merged PR #14 (6f2b41a): shared packages/core/src/jsonPointer.ts; resolveRefs.ts + ajvValidator.ts migrated; allOf-walk half deferred to jsonschema-form-0z9.

title: Evaluate Standard Schema alignment for the Validator seam
description: Standard Schema (standardschema.dev) standardizes a validator's issues as {message, path} (no keyword vocabulary). Evaluate aligning our Validator contract to the Standard Schema interface so Zod/Valibot/ArkType plug in natively. Relates to keyword-normalization bead jsonschema-form-1oz. Raised by maintainer on PR #2.
notes: ADR 026 (Proposed): speak Standard Schema at the boundary, don't replace the contract. Tracer in PR #15 — toStandardSchema/fromStandardSchema in core (inlined v1 types, zero-dep) + 10 tests, gate green. Awaiting review/merge.
close_reason: Merged PR #15 (c4f1baa, 0076ee0): ADR 026 — adapt Standard Schema at the boundary (toStandardSchema/fromStandardSchema in core), not adopt; reason is the dot-string node.path/issue.path that powers typed FieldPath. Follow-up: 1d3 (App_12 simplification).

title: React: JSX re-entry component layer (<Default of/> / <Children of/>) over callable handles
close_reason: Implemented in ADR 017: stable Default/Children components delegating to node callables (ADR 016); RenderNode -> (node, helpers); IOC injection + export; unified node/child/part, null-safe, typed parts. Gate green; conformance matches vanilla oracle; App_08 migrated + browser-verified.

title: React fold remounted nodes on real re-render (fresh-closure <node.Default/>)
close_reason: Fixed: React fold now renders by calling continuation handles (node.Default()/part.Default()) through stable module-level components (NodeRenderer/ArrayRoot/PartHost) + new renderPart engine seam. Real re-renders reconcile in place; no remount. ADR 016.

title: Densify array submission (sparse FormData -> dense array)
description: Array items keep stable, non-contiguous paths (e.g. contacts.1 survives after removing contacts.0) to preserve React identity/values (ADR 015). unflatten() currently yields a SPARSE array with holes. Decide + implement densification: compact in unflatten, or remap a display-index at submit. Crosses Core submit semantics - decide deliberately.
close_reason: Dense array submission solved via UI re-pathing (ADR 018), not a Core/unflatten change. React combine keys siblings by position (decoupling React identity from form path); ArrayRoot re-paths survivors densely event-time in the state updater. Removing the first of two items now submits a contiguous array { contacts: [{...}] } with no leading hole, value preserved in place. Verified by arrays.test.tsx (dense submit), render-counts.test.tsx (re-render contract), and live App_07 sanity.

title: Cross-framework HTML/CSS conformance suite (golden-DOM oracle)
description:
A framework-agnostic golden-DOM fixture set: (schema [+overrides]) -> normalized HTML. Each UI adapter (React today; vanilla/Vue next) renders the fixtures; Playwright normalizes the DOM (strip framework noise) and diffs against the golden. This becomes the gate that lets us swap UI frameworks safely while leaning on verification (ADR 009).

Reinforces ADR 012 §3 (Core owns the native HTML attrs) — identical output across frameworks is only possible if the structure/attrs are a shared contract. The eventual vanilla (no-framework) renderer likely doubles as the canonical oracle that produces the golden HTML. Open question (ADR 013): whether the default node-template *arrangement* should be a shared framework-agnostic descriptor vs. idiomatic-per-framework templates kept in sync by this suite.

title: Build and package all maintained workspaces for external consumption
description: Replace placeholder build scripts and source-file exports across maintained packages with real distributable artifacts. Produce ESM/CJS as justified, declaration files, correct package exports/files metadata, and an external-consumer smoke test using packed tarballs. Cover Core, input packages, React, vanilla, and maintained validation packages rather than Core alone.
design:
Choose the lightest build tool that handles the monorepo. Branding/package-scope changes are a separate decision bead (5ss.7), but the build must not hard-code assumptions that make a rename costly.

--- GRILL DECISIONS (2026-07-10) ---
1. Formats: dual ESM + CJS (tsup emits both in one pass). Dual-package hazard is inert here — Core is stateless; only React context is identity-sensitive and an app resolves one format.
2. Build tool: tsup per package (esbuild). dts pass points at a non-composite tsconfig (tsconfig.tsup.json, composite:false) to dodge TS6307 from the repo's composite project refs.
3. Dev-resolution seam: a "development" export condition -> src, plus root tsconfig customConditions:["development"] and vitest resolve.conditions. Keeps the fast gate BUILD-FREE. Proven: dependent typecheck + tests pass with core/dist deleted.
4. Published exports: types/import/require -> dist (index.js ESM, index.cjs CJS, index.d.ts/.d.cts), files:["dist"], type:module.
5. Publish scope: ALL nine packages, nothing private. core, input-jsonschema, input-zod, input-conformance, react, vanilla, validation-ajv, validation-zod, validation-contract. vanilla is a real product that also serves as the conformance oracle. Both contract suites (validation-contract, input-conformance) are published because customizability is a first-class axis — third parties hand-write input + validation adapters and need the oracles to conform.
6. Smoke test: separate CI job "build-and-smoke" (NOT folded into the fast gate). Build all -> npm pack the 9 -> temp external consumer installs the tarballs -> (a) consumer tsc typechecks imports from every package, (b) dual ESM+CJS runtime: jsonSchemaToTree + zodToTree + vanilla renderToString assert HTML, (c) React import/types only (browser render already covered by react's Playwright tests). Proves dist exports/types resolve AND that the development condition does NOT leak (no src shipped).
7. Rename-proofing (guards 5ss.7): build config is name-agnostic; only the smoke consumer's import strings reference package names, and those ride the rename PR.

REWORK ASSESSMENT: low. Build wiring is orthogonal to API churn (bundles whatever src exports) and to the rename (name-agnostic). Real payoff (external install) is deferred to actual publish; doing it now buys a continuous external-consumer reality check that de-risks the in-flight DX work.
acceptance_criteria: npm run build creates clean dist artifacts for every maintained package; declarations resolve; package exports do not point at src; npm pack contents are correct; a temporary external consumer can install tarballs, typecheck, and run a minimal form compilation/render import; the gate stays green.
close_reason: Merged in PR #54 — dual ESM+CJS build for all 9 packages, external-consumer smoke test (publint+attw), CI build-and-smoke job, ADR 036.

title: Add array field support (repeating fields)
Add enum/select field type support
Add boolean field type support (checkboxes)
Design: explicit value-normalization seam at FormData->data boundary (empty-string policy)
description:
## Context
Core's submit currently applies a hidden, global transform: `omitEmptyFormValues` (packages/core/src/parser/groupNode.submitUtils.ts, called from groupNode.ts ~line 170) strips empty-string FormData entries so that native-form values line up with schema "required = key absent" semantics. Discovered during 9jk async-validation work: this transform interacts surprisingly with validation (a blank field arrives as `undefined` -> Zod `invalid_type` -> aborts object-level `.refine`s), which confused example 16.

## The question (from Tim)
Should we revert the ""->undefined conversion (let validators/consumers handle it, like RHF's per-field setValueAs / TanStack's manual model), or add an IOC seam so end-devs can hook the value pipeline (transform "" -> undefined, trim, number coercion, per-field keep-empty, etc.)?

## Findings / tradeoffs
- **Do NOT simply revert.** Native `<form>`/FormData always yields "" for empty text inputs. Without the strip, `z.object({ email: z.string() })` sees `email: ""` (key present) so required/missing-key never fires; `.optional()` treats "" as present. Reverting globally breaks out-of-the-box native-form + schema "required" for every consumer and changes a golden scenario => stop/escalate + ADR, not a quiet revert.
- **"RHF/TanStack handle it" is only partial.** RHF offers per-field setValueAs/valueAsNumber/shouldUnregister (not a global default); TanStack stores raw values (no auto-convert). So dropping our default just pushes a per-field papercut onto consumers.
- **Current placement is arguably wrong layer.** The normalization lives in Core's submit; it's really a consumer/adapter concern. Core could assemble raw data and expose a normalization slot at the React (or form-state) boundary (relates to ADR 011 form-state-as-shallow-slot).

## Proposed direction
1. Make the transform **explicit and documented** now (it's currently invisible; ADR 018 already carves the sparse-array exception).
2. Design an **IOC value-normalization seam** (e.g. `useFormTree(tree, { normalizeValues })` or a small composable pipeline) with the current omit-empty policy as the default, overridable/removable (keep-empty, trim, number coercion, per-field rules).
3. Per ADR 008, earn the seam with a **second** concrete transform need (trim / number coercion / a field that legitimately keeps ""). Co-design with the **RHF bridge (5ss.9)**, which will want to own value handling — good forcing function for the seam shape.

## Acceptance / next step
- Write an ADR proposing the seam (or explicitly deciding to keep the baked-in default) once a second transform need is concrete.
- Until then: keep current behavior; document it in README/architecture as an intentional normalization step.

title: Builder-stability gaps: non-hook renderNodeRules() inline-builder remount (no warning); useCallback stable-identity-but-changed-deps silently freezes rules

ValidationSummary lists errors in validator order, not field/DOM order
description:
## Problem
`ValidationSummary` renders errors in whatever order the validator/ErrorStore produced them (e.g. Zod/Standard Schema issue order), NOT the order the fields appear in the form tree / on the page. This makes the summary's in-page links feel scrambled relative to the visible field order, especially with nested groups or arrays.

## Repro
- examples/basic-react example 16 (async validation), or any multi-field form with `<ValidationSummary />`.
- Submit with multiple fields invalid.
- Observe the summary list order can differ from the top-to-bottom field order on the page.

## Expected
Summary entries appear in the same order the fields are rendered (tree walk / DOM order), so the list reads top-to-bottom like the form.

## Actual
Order follows validator issue emission order (`useValidationErrors()` returns errors as stored in ErrorStore), which is independent of field layout.

## Cause
`packages/react/src/ValidationSummary.tsx` maps `useValidationErrors()` directly. The ErrorStore preserves insertion/validator order; there is no re-ordering against the form tree's field order.

## Suggested fix
Order the summary by each error's field position in the presented tree. Options:
- Compute a stable field-order index by walking the tree (e.g. `form.getAllFields()` / walk order) and sort/group errors by that index before rendering.
- Handle multiple errors per path and paths not present in the tree (fallback to append-at-end, stable).
- Keep it O(n) with a path→index map built once per render from the tree.

Add a test asserting summary order matches tree/field order for a form whose validator emits errors out of order.

title: Docs + example: async validation (pending, failure, stale-result)
description: Product-facing documentation and a runnable example for the async validation feature shipped in 9jk (PR #71). Cover: passing an AsyncValidator (or createZodAsyncValidator / fromStandardSchemaAsync) to useFormTree; the pending signals via useIsValidating/useIsSubmitting (e.g. a spinner + disabled submit); the run-failure surface via useValidationFailure (validator threw/rejected != invalid verdict); stale-result protection behavior (retained errors while revalidating; newest run wins); and async onValid (isSubmitting spans it). Add an example app under examples/basic-react demonstrating an async (e.g. debounced remote uniqueness) check. Distinct from 5ss.5 (the general validation decision guide) — this is async-specific teaching. Naming ratification note: implementation chose useIsValidating/useIsSubmitting/useValidationFailure/FormStoreProvider — the 9jk.1 map deferred these public names to the DX epic; confirm or rename here.

title: Document/enforce as-const requirement: error when S resolves to bare JSONSchema

Document the as-const requirement AND ideally surface a compile error when S resolves to exactly JSONSchema (no literal info).

Migrated ~35 runtime call sites (tests/examples) to the runtime door; typed-binding showcases stay on jsonSchemaToTree with as const. Added asConstGuard.test.ts (@ts-expect-error pins the guard). Tighten both so widget/description narrowing matches Core's runtime derivation.

(2) HasDescription now handles widened/branded/union strings and excludes empty-string literals. New/updated assertions in infer.control.test.ts (both front-ends) and formShape.conformance.test.tsx. Gate green.

title: Unified submit-outcome API shape and no-error-UI dev safeguards
description:
## Context

ADR 042 fixed validation-run failure *semantics* (authoritative failure retains `errors`, exposes the raw reason on a separate authority-tracked surface) and the *no-silent-submit* guarantee (invalid publishes `errors`, failure publishes the failure surface, `submitted` always latches). It deliberately deferred the public-API shape questions to the v1 DX epic.

## Decide here

- Whether to add a unified `onSubmit(outcome)` tagged-union callback (`valid` / `invalid` / `failed`) alongside or instead of the success-only `onValid`. Trade-off: TS exhaustiveness vs. taxing the 90% success case with branching and duplicating info already in observable state.
- Public *names* for the run-failure surface (and pending surface, coordinated with 9jk.1.5).
- A sensible **default for the failure surface**: log an unhandled run failure to `console.error` so a thrown validator / network error is never swallowed.
- A **dev-mode warning** for the "consumer never rendered an error surface" perceptual no-op that the no-silent-submit guarantee cannot itself prevent.

## Out of scope

- Run authority / staleness / failure *semantics* — settled by ADR 042.

title: Ratify the async validation design
description:
## Question

Does the combined seam, ordering, pending, submit, failure, interop, and conformance design form one implementation-ready decision with no unresolved behavior before coding begins?

## Required Skills

notes: HITL final ratification. Consolidate an ADR-grade resolution and implementation test matrix; do not implement the feature.
close_reason: Final ratification complete (ADR 046): ADRs 041-045 compose into one implementation-ready design with no unresolved behavior. Reconciled the three useFormTree counters (generation=publication rights; isValidating/isSubmitting=observable ref-counts) and the canonical submit lifecycle (superseded submit suppresses errors via gate yet still fires onValid with click-time data). Fixed the implementation test matrix across seam/authority/pending/retained-errors/submit/failure/conformance/cancellation. Only deferred item is public surface names (5ss), not a blocker. Design handed to the Validation implementation track.

title: Define Standard Schema and conformance evolution
description:
## Question

How should fromStandardSchema, synchronous adapters, optional async adapters, and the shared validation-contract suite evolve to express the chosen async interface and ordering invariants without source-origin sniffing; specifically, does the maintained Zod adapter need a direct async factory to avoid Zod `~standard.validate` per-call sync-then-async duplicate execution?

## Required Skills
grilling,domain-modeling,codebase-design
notes: HITL grilling. Keep Standard Schema validation-only and preserve synchronous source compatibility.
close_reason: Async Standard-Schema interop + conformance evolution ratified in ADR 045: direct async Zod factory createZodAsyncValidator over safeParseAsync (forced — sync safeParse throws on async schemas; preserves keyword; single execution avoids sync-then-async double-run); async sibling bridges fromStandardSchemaAsync/toStandardSchemaAsync mirroring ADR 041 (sync bridges unchanged); no source-origin sniffing (consumer picks sync/async factory; useFormTree branches on result Promise-shape); contract suite gains async execution mode running same per-call invariants, ADR 042 ordering/staleness stays in orchestration tests; no data->value rename. Names deferred to DX epic.

title: Define submit snapshots and transformed-output behavior
description:
## Question

Which FormData snapshot does async submit validate, when may transformed ValidationResult data reach onValid, and what happens if values change or another validation run starts before submit resolves?

## Required Skills
grilling,domain-modeling
notes: HITL grilling. Preserve the existing transformed-output and validator-purity contracts.
close_reason: Submit snapshot + transformed-output mechanics ratified in ADR 043: click-time snapshot (never resolve-time re-read); onValid gets result.data else snapshot only on the submit run's own valid verdict; submit run dual-natured (errors/failure gated by ADR 042 generation counter, onValid ungated by authority); double-submit allowed with no library gating; native form-state exposes reference-counted isSubmitting spanning the async onValid (onValid becomes awaitable); isSubmitting distinct from 9jk.1.5 validation-pending and is the native floor ceded to RHF/TanStack. Names + unified onSubmit + callback-failure affordance deferred to DX epic.

title: Define pending and retained-error behavior
description:
## Question

What pending state is observable at form and field scope, and should previously authoritative errors remain visible, clear, or become stale-marked while a newer validation run is pending?

## Required Skills
prototype,domain-modeling,react-best-practices
notes: HITL prototype. Keep concrete visual styling out of scope; resolve behavior and information ownership.
close_reason: Pending + retained-error behavior ratified in ADR 044: stale-while-revalidate (errors held until resolve, never blanked at start); resolve replaces verdict per-path diff-wise (ADR 023 stable refs); staleness is presentation composition over errors+isValidating, not a data state; isValidating is a form-scope reference-counted any-origin 'verdict in flight' boolean (isSubmitting is its time-superset), no per-field pending (whole-document); ALL form/validation state lives in the store read via useSyncExternalStore selectors (resolves hook-vs-store toward store); refines ADR 027 so the submit reveal is O(errored fields) not O(fields). Names deferred to DX epic.

title: Define validation-run authority, cancellation, and failure semantics
description:
## Question

Which validation run is authoritative across live input and submit, when does a result become stale, what roles do generation checks and cooperative cancellation play, and how are thrown or rejected validators represented?

## Required Skills
grilling,domain-modeling
notes: HITL grilling. Stress-test live-vs-live, live-vs-submit, double-submit, and unmount scenarios.
close_reason: Validation-run authority (one shared-state track, start-order generation counter, supersede-on-start staleness, single per-run gate over all channels), cancellation out of scope/non-load-bearing, and failure semantics (retain errors + separate authority-tracked raw-reason surface; submit never calls onValid without a valid verdict; no-silent-submit guarantee) ratified in ADR 042. DX API-shape follow-ups split to jsonschema-form-8f6.

title: Choose the async Validator seam
description:
## Question

Should async validation evolve Validator to return a value or Promise, introduce a sibling seam, or use another interface; and what canonical validation-run vocabulary and compatibility invariants must that interface establish?

## Required Skills

title: Map async validation semantics
description:
## Destination

Reach an implementation-ready decision for evolving the Validator seam while preserving synchronous callers, transformed submit output, deterministic stale-result handling, explicit pending and failure semantics, Standard Schema interop, and existing render-stability contracts. The result hands a resolved design back to Validation: async Validator support with stale-result protection for implementation.

## Notes

Domain: FormFrame validation. Each session should consult the codebase-design, domain-modeling, and React best-practices skills. Work against origin/main. Current constraints: Validator is synchronous; useFormTree owns orchestration; validation is whole-document; ErrorStore preserves per-path reference stability; data carries transformed output; errors is the canonical owned term. This map makes decisions only.

## Decisions so far

- [Survey upstream async validation contracts](beads://jsonschema-form-9jk.1.1) — Schema contracts define per-call sync/async results and transformed output; stale authority, pending publication, and optional cooperative cancellation belong to form orchestration.
- [Exercise async validation race scenarios](beads://jsonschema-form-9jk.1.2) — Newest-started runs own shared validation state; submit callbacks retain click-time snapshot independence; rejected runs retain errors and surface failure separately.
- [Choose the async Validator seam](beads://jsonschema-form-9jk.1.3) — Preserve synchronous `Validator`; add Promise-returning sibling `AsyncValidator`; accept either through the single `useFormTree({ validator })` slot; preserve immediate sync publication; keep `ValidationResult` unchanged and treat throws/rejections as validation-run failures outside the verdict.
- [Define validation-run authority, cancellation, and failure semantics](beads://jsonschema-form-9jk.1.4) — One shared-state authority track owned by the newest-*started* run (submit callbacks judged on their own click-time result); authority is a start-order generation counter in useFormTree; staleness is supersede-on-start (publish only if generation still current); one generation gate governs errors, failure, and settle together; cancellation is out of scope and non-load-bearing (aborted collapses into stale=silent, stale runs discarded not stored); authoritative failure retains errors and exposes the raw reason on a separate authority-tracked surface; submit failure never calls onValid, `submitted` always latches, and every submit attempt drives observable state (no-silent-submit guarantee). Recorded in ADR 042.
- [Define submit snapshots and transformed-output behavior](beads://jsonschema-form-9jk.1.6) — Submit validates the click-time assembled snapshot (never a resolve-time re-read); onValid receives result.data (else the snapshot) only on the submit run's own valid verdict; the submit run is dual-natured — errors/failure publish through the shared generation gate (superseded ⇒ suppressed) while onValid is ungated by authority (a superseded submit still fires onValid with click-time data); double-submit is allowed with no library gating (each submit independent, own snapshot, own onValid), so the native form-state exposes a reference-counted `isSubmitting` (count > 0) whose window spans the async onValid — making onValid awaitable, `(data) => void | Promise<void>`; onValid rejection clears isSubmitting but is not routed through the ADR 042 failure surface; isSubmitting is distinct from validation-pending (9jk.1.5) and is the native form-state floor, ceded to RHF/TanStack when they own the slot. Recorded in ADR 043.
- [Define pending and retained-error behavior](beads://jsonschema-form-9jk.1.5) — Stale-while-revalidate: retained errors stay visible unchanged until the pending run resolves and replaces the verdict (never blanked at start); resolve writes the whole-document verdict per-path diff-wise (ADR 023 stable refs) so only genuinely-changed fields re-render (success ⇒ shared empty ref, invalid ⇒ new set with unchanged paths keeping refs, failure ⇒ errors retained + failure surface). Staleness is a consumer/presentation composition over `errors + isValidating`, not a data state. `isValidating` is a form-scope, reference-counted, any-origin "verdict in flight" boolean (isSubmitting is its time-superset through onValid); no per-field pending (whole-document, ADR 028). Ownership resolved toward the store: ALL form/validation state (per-path errors/touched, form-scope submitted, isSubmitting, isValidating) lives in the ADR 023 store read via useSyncExternalStore selector hooks — nothing in broadcast context. Refines ADR 027: submitted folds into per-field display selectors so the submit reveal costs O(errored fields), not O(fields); the invariant is that only components whose rendered output changed re-render. Recorded in ADR 044.

- [Define Standard Schema and conformance evolution](beads://jsonschema-form-9jk.1.7) — Add a direct async Zod factory `createZodAsyncValidator` over safeParseAsync (forced: sync safeParse throws on async schemas; preserves keyword the generic Standard hop drops; single execution, so no sync-then-async double-run). Mirror the ADR 041 sibling seam at the Standard boundary: `fromStandardSchemaAsync` (consume any async Standard lib as AsyncValidator, awaits ~standard.validate uniformly) and `toStandardSchemaAsync` (emit AsyncValidator as async Standard Schema); sync fromStandardSchema/toStandardSchema unchanged (throw on Promise). No source-origin sniffing: consumer explicitly picks sync vs async factory; useFormTree's single slot takes Validator | AsyncValidator and branches on result *shape* (Promise?) at call time. Contract suite (ADR 020) gains an async execution mode running the same per-call invariants (purity, no-aliasing, verdict, transformed value) by awaiting; ADR 042 ordering/staleness stays in useFormTree orchestration tests, not the per-call suite; async Standard conformance runs the suite over fromStandardSchemaAsync + createZodAsyncValidator. No data->value rename (ADR 026 holds). Recorded in ADR 045.

- [Ratify the async validation design](beads://jsonschema-form-9jk.1.8) — Final ratification: ADRs 041–045 compose into one implementation-ready design with no unresolved behavior. Reconciles the three useFormTree counters (generation = publication rights; isValidating/isSubmitting = observable ref-counted booleans) and the canonical submit lifecycle where 042/043/044 act at once (superseded submit suppresses errors via the gate yet still fires onValid with click-time data). Fixes the implementation test matrix across seam, authority/staleness, pending, retained errors, submit, failure, conformance, and cancellation. Only deferred item: public surface names (5ss), not a blocker. Recorded in ADR 046. **The 9jk.1 map is fully resolved.**

## Resolved

All children 9jk.1.1–9jk.1.8 closed; async validation design handed to the Validation implementation track (ADR 046 test matrix is the done-oracle).

## Not yet specified

- Exact public names for the pending-state (`isValidating`), run-failure, and `isSubmitting` surfaces (converge in DX epic).

## Out of scope

- Implementing the async validation feature before this map is complete.
- Built-in debounce; consumer-owned wrapping remains the default unless evidence redraws the destination.
- Field-scoped validation, already deferred by ADR 028.
- Concrete spinner, disabled-button, and other visual styling.
- General composition of local and remote validators.
- Submit-callback API shape (unified onSubmit), failure-surface default logging, submit-callback-failure affordance, and dev-mode no-error-UI warning — split to DX follow-up jsonschema-form-8f6.
notes: Planning child of Validation: async Validator support with stale-result protection. Open child issues form the frontier; resolved decisions are indexed here by name.

title: Rename rendering packages to renderer-react and renderer-vanilla
description: Align the rendering adapter family with FormFrame's capability-prefixed package naming. Rename @formframe/react and @formframe/vanilla to @formframe/renderer-react and @formframe/renderer-vanilla across manifests, imports, docs, examples, lockfile, and smoke tests. Reserve ui-* terminology for actual presentation/UI-kit adapters so package names preserve the architecture's distinction between framework rendering and presentation.

title: Ratify shared conformance oracles across sister adapters
description:
Record and enforce the repository-wide testing rule for sister adapter packages: wherever capabilities overlap, every adapter in the family runs the same exhaustive central oracle/contract suite; adapter-local tests cover only genuine source-language or implementation-specific behavior.

The code already follows this for validation-* via @jsonschema-form/validation-contract and for input-* via @jsonschema-form/input-conformance, but only validation has a dedicated ADR. Input conformance is implemented and cited to ADRs 033/034 without those ADRs actually recording the shared test mechanism.

Concrete audit findings:
- Promote the pre-existing numeric choice-set behavior (JSON numeric enum / Zod numeric literal union) into input-conformance because both sisters already assert the same neutral result locally.
- Evaluate exporting the existing neutral node assertion from input-conformance for reuse by source-specific degradation tests; do so only where at least two packages consume it.
- Remove local tests only after the shared oracle covers the same invariant.
- Do not force genuinely source-specific behavior (JSON combinator/rejection policy, Zod wrapper/degradation semantics) into lockstep.
design: Add a small ADR dedicated to shared contract suites per adapter family rather than overloading ADR 008 (when seams are earned), ADR 020 (validation-specific extraction), or ADRs 033/034 (input compiler architecture). The decision should distinguish overlapping family contracts from adapter-specific behavior and preserve the one-way dependency: neutral test package knows no concrete adapters; each adapter supplies only its fixtures/target.
acceptance_criteria:
- An ADR states that overlapping behavior in a sister adapter family is asserted by one central test-only oracle/runner/assertion set once a second real adapter exists.
- The ADR states that each adapter supplies only source/implementation-specific fixtures and keeps local tests only for genuine differences.
- The ADR cites validation-contract and input-conformance as current implementations and renderer conformance as the same pattern where applicable.
- input-conformance gains a numeric choice-set scenario supplied exhaustively by JSON Schema and Zod.
- Any exported shared assertion helper has at least two concrete consumers; otherwise it remains private.
- Existing local duplicate tests are removed only when the oracle provides equivalent coverage.
- Input, validation, and full gate suites pass.
close_reason: ADR 038 ratifies shared sister-adapter oracles; numeric choices now run through the exhaustive input oracle for JSON Schema and Zod.

title: Decide v1 schema-agnostic product and package naming
description:
The architecture and product are schema-agnostic, while the repository, npm scope, metadata, and search terms still say JSON Schema. Decide whether v1 keeps jsonschema-form with explicit schema-agnostic positioning or migrates toward schemaform or another name, then define package-scope, compatibility, migration, and discovery consequences.

## Rationale
The umbrella name affects who discovers the project, what Zod-like users expect, package publication, migration cost, and whether the public identity matches the architecture. This should be an explicit human product decision before stable publication.

## Alternatives Considered
1. Keep jsonschema-form and make schema-agnostic positioning unmistakable.
2. Rename the umbrella project and npm scope toward schemaform while retaining input-jsonschema as a front-end package.
3. Publish aliases or a compatibility scope during migration.
4. Defer the rename until after v1, accepting a larger future migration.
design: This is a human product decision, not a mechanical rename. Preserve input-jsonschema as a clear front-end name regardless of the umbrella brand.
acceptance_criteria: An ADR records the chosen product name and npm scope; search/discovery tradeoffs for JSON Schema and Zod-like audiences are considered; migration or alias strategy is explicit; package metadata and documentation changes are enumerated; the decision occurs before stable package publication.
notes: Decision: human-facing product name FormFrame; npm scope @formframe. Keep source-specific suffixes such as input-jsonschema. Update PR #56; repository slug rename remains a separate GitHub operation.
close_reason: Chosen FormFrame as the product name and @formframe as the package scope; recorded discovery, migration, and repository-slug consequences in ADR 038 and implemented in PR #56.

title: Add an evidence-backed Zod input support catalog
description: Mirror the JSON Schema support-catalog discipline for the maintained Zod front-end. Enumerate structurally supported Zod types and wrappers, qualified or degraded behavior, validation-only semantics, rejected inputs, default presentation outcomes, and direct-introspection compatibility assumptions.

They are defined in renderer.tsx and described as importable, but the package root does not export them. title: Thread schema type S through WalkHandlers and continuation types
description: From B3 review (ADR 033 §4). origin.schema is typed via NodeFacts<S>, but two surfaces erase S back to unknown: (1) WalkHandlers<R> node params are FieldNode/GroupNode/etc (= <unknown>) in nodeTypes.ts — a consumer walking a GroupNode<JSONSchemaObject> gets unknown origin.schema in callbacks. (2) continuation ENode/EField/EGroup/EArray (engine.ts) use FieldNode etc without S. Make WalkHandlers<R, S=unknown> and thread S through the continuation node types so consumers get typed origin.schema in walk/render. Keep S=unknown default for back-compat.
close_reason: WalkHandlers and continuation types thread S; gate green

title: Extract @jsonschema-form/input-jsonschema; decouple Core (PR B)
description: PR B of the Core-decoupling / Zod-front-end plan. Extract all JSON-Schema-specific parsing into a new @jsonschema-form/input-jsonschema package; Core keeps only the neutral tree + present() + submit and imports nothing schema-specific. Includes: (1) neutral builders (createFieldNode/createGroupNode/createArrayNode/createArrayItemNode) that accept already-neutral inputs, with createArrayNode taking an itemFactory closure (index)=>AnyNode; (2) make origin.schema a generic type param S=unknown and drop NodeBase.schema; (3) RELOCATE the scalar-choice-array collapse parser->present as a DEFAULT (ADR 030 §3 amendment) so front-ends are pure structural transcribers; (4) REMOVE the redundant node.validation field (move minItems/maxItems into facts.constraints) so the minItems->minLength wart dies as the collapse relocates (pinned today by parser.test.ts 'respects minItems and maxItems for multiselect'). Enables PR C (input-zod, 4qe) to inherit collapse for free.

title: Uniform spreadable attrs per part + single neutral→framework translator
description: PR #36 review (comments 1,2,4). Generalize the l8j 'role into Core' + '{id, for?} spreadable label' moves into one principle: EVERY renderable part exposes its DOM attributes as one spreadable neutral attrs bag, and each adapter has exactly ONE neutral->framework translator (for->htmlFor, class->className, etc.) applied to any bag. No consumer hand-picks individual attributes. Concretely: (a) choicegroup control exposes wrapperAttrs { role, 'aria-labelledby' } to spread onto the wrapper <div> instead of consumers placing role/aria-labelledby by hand (demoting control.multiple to informational metadata); (b) a shared htmlAttrsToReactProps() helper in the React adapter; (c) audit every part (input/select/textarea/label/option/addButton/wrapper) for a consistent spreadable attrs surface. Likely warrants an ADR (public parts-contract change generalizing bd l8j). The minimal label {id, for?} slice already shipped in #36; this is the full generalization.

title: Root getAllFields() skips fields inside array subtrees
description: PR #33 (7mr) + groupNode.ts getAllFields recurse only into child GROUPS, not array item subtrees, so leaves inside dynamic arrays are omitted from the root's flat field list. Consumers needing a complete flat leaf list with arrays present must fall back to walk({ field }). Decide: make getAllFields include array-item leaves, add an opt-in ({ includeArrayItems: true }), or document the limitation. Pairs with the getField-through-arrays gap.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #40 (ADR 032). GroupNode.getAllFields now recurses into array children, so array-item leaves are included — getAllFields() ≡ walk({ field }). Covered by deepNesting.test.ts ('getAllFields includes every array-item leaf').

title: Root getField() cannot resolve paths through array indices
description: PR #33 (7mr) tests document that form.getField('org.department.members.0.name') from the ROOT group returns undefined — fields inside dynamic arrays are only reachable via relative getField on the item group or via walk(). Relative-path resolution is ADR-documented, but absolute lookup through numeric array indices is a real query gap for consumers that only hold the root node. Decide whether root getField should traverse array item children (e.g. 'members.0.name') and implement, or document the limitation explicitly in the API.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #40 (ADR 032). Root/group getField now recurses into array children; numeric path segments select array items by index (e.g. members.0.name, chained as teams.0.members.1.name). ArrayNode/ArrayItemNode.getField realigned to the relative-path convention. Resolution ranges over the instantiated tree; getItem(i) is the escape hatch for not-yet-added items. Covered by deepNesting.test.ts.

title: choicegroup caption a11y: aria-labelledby wrapper instead of label-for-first-option
description: PR #31 (cm7) review: for a radio/checkbox group the caption <label for> is retargeted at the FIRST option's id (present.ts deriveFieldParts). That programmatically associates the caption but has a surprising side effect: clicking the caption activates/selects the first radio. The canonical grouping patterns are (a) fieldset+legend, or (b) role=radiogroup|group on the wrapper + aria-labelledby pointing at the caption id. Proposal: extend the neutral a11y contract so a choicegroup emits a caption id + wrapper aria-labelledby (Core-derived, so React==vanilla), and drop the label-for retarget. This is an ADR-029 a11y-contract addition (label part gains an id; control/wrapper gains aria-labelledby), touching both adapters + oracles + conformance. Deferred out of cm7 to keep that PR to the widget/heuristic slice.

title: Implement the ADR 029 §5 unified field.control render slot
description: The single render slot that lets any widget (radio, checkbox-group, textarea, collapsed multiselect) render through one path instead of the closed input|select|multiselect dispatch in DefaultFieldRoot. Unblocks cm7 (radio/checkbox/textarea + heuristic), 9el (textarea), kon (tags/pills), and the RENDERING half of ADR 030 (fcj) collapsed containers. Core FieldNode union + engine FieldPartRenderers must open to a general control descriptor; React DefaultFieldRoot dispatches on it. This is the highest-leverage next step: it is the shared prerequisite for the whole widget-catalog expansion and for seeing ADR 030 collapse on screen.
close_reason: Merged as PR #30 — unified field.control render slot (ADR 029 §5) + textarea; clean break, gate green.

title: ADR: present()/renderNode layer boundary — widgets are (visual+value-contract) pairs
close_reason: ADR 031 merged (PR #29)

title: radio/textarea/checkbox widgets need the ADR 029 §5 field.control render slot
description:
Deferred from 4j1. Needs the ADR 029 §5 unified field.control render slot: today React's DefaultFieldRoot dispatches only input vs select, and the engine's FieldPartRenderers + Core's FieldNode union are closed to input|select|multiselect. Adding radio/checkbox-group/textarea requires that slot.

WIDGETS: radio (single-select, small N), checkbox group (multi-select, small N), textarea (long/multiline string).

DEFAULT-PRESENTATION HEURISTIC (option-count driven; agreed w/ Tim 2026-07-02) — all remain overridable by a consumer resolver (ADR 029):
- scalar + choices:   length <= 5 -> radio;   6-15 -> select;   16+ -> (future) combobox/typeahead.
- array + choices (multiple):   length <= 5 -> checkbox group;   else -> multiselect (<select multiple>).
- string w/ long/multiline hint (large maxLength or a textarea format) -> textarea (orthogonal to the count heuristic).
Put the threshold in ONE named constant (start at 5) so it is tunable.

WHY 5: convergent UX research. USWDS / VA.gov design system: 2-5 radio, 6-15 select, 16-100 combobox, 101+ search. GoogleChrome modern-web-guidance forms matrix: 1-5 radio, 6+ select, 10+ combobox. Queensland Gov: max 6 radios (Miller 7±2) else select. Setproduct / Cieden: 2-6 radios, 7+ dropdown. 5 is the conservative common boundary.

Note: enum AND oneOf both already produce neutral 'choices' (fieldNode + arrayNode), so the heuristic keys purely off choices.length + valueShape — no schema-keyword coupling.
close_reason: Implemented radio/checkbox-group widgets via the unified field.control 'choicegroup' archetype + option-count heuristic (OPTION_COUNT_THRESHOLD=5) in present(); wired React + vanilla (string/DOM) renderers, examples, and ADR 029; gate green.

title: Expand the default widget catalog (checkbox, radio, date, textarea, etc.)
description:
Follow-up to the wsr tracer (ADR 029). defaultPresentation + the Core widget catalog are built to GROW; this bead adds the common form controls beyond input/select/multiselect.

Two sub-cases the seam already handles:
1. SAME facts, many widgets — e.g. a boolean can be a checkbox, a Yes/No radio, a Yes/No select, or a custom push-button. Identical FieldFacts; the resolver (or a new default rule) picks the widget. This is the canonical case the present() seam was designed for.
2. NEW facts drive NEW defaults — e.g. date keys off facts.format==='date' (facts ALREADY carry format), so many are just new catalog entries + default rules with NO facts change.

Candidate archetypes: checkbox (boolean), radio group (enum, small N), textarea (string + long/format hint), date/datetime/time (format), number stepper, range/slider (min/max), color, file. Each = a neutral deriveXParts(facts) in the Core catalog (shared with the string oracle so React ≡ vanilla holds) + a defaultPresentation rule + conformance coverage.

Pure additive; do AFTER 9pb closes the dual period (present() as sole widget/parts source) OR carefully keep parser + present() in sync while the dual period is open.

title: Generalize the neutral waist to containers (NodeFacts) so present() can collapse an array/object subtree into a single widget
description:
Follow-up surfaced by the wsr tracer (ADR 029) + the VNDLY object-array multiselect case.

TODAY: FieldFacts exists only on LEAF nodes (FieldNode). A subtree array (type:array, items:object) becomes an ArrayNode with NO facts, so a PresentationResolver cannot opt it into a single collapsed widget. Leaf enum-arrays get valueShape:'array' + inline choices; subtree arrays get nothing. Object subtrees (GroupNode) likewise have no facts — this absorbs the deferred valueShape:'object' question from ADR 029.

GOAL: generalize FieldFacts into a NodeFacts waist that also projects CONTAINER nodes, so present() can collapse an array/object subtree into one leaf-like widget when a resolver asks for it.

KEY DESIGN POINTS:
- valueShape is the shape of the SUBMITTED value, independent of whether the node is currently decomposed. A leaf enum-array and a collapsed object-array BOTH get valueShape:'array' and stay consistent. Default rule keys multiselect off valueShape==='array' && choices, so a subtree array (no choices) correctly stays add/remove until a resolver overrides it.
- Multiselect-over-object-array is RICHER than the enum case: it needs (a) an option SOURCE (often async/remote), and (b) a value identity (each selection is an object {name,type} — which field is value, which is label). That is NOT 'choices'; it is 'args' ({ optionsSource, valueKey, labelKey }). Same widget name, divergent facts/args — exactly why args exists.
- Container facts likely carry valueShape + EITHER choices (finite) OR an element/item descriptor (open-ended); resolver supplies the option source via args.
- present() must be able to collapse: prune the subtree's children and emit a single control node, while submit assembly still produces the array value.

Reference (VNDLY primary multiselect pattern): allowed_criteria: { type:'array', items:{ type:'object', properties:{ name:{type:string}, type:{type:string} } } }.

Depends conceptually on the field.control facet / catalog work (9pb-adjacent) for how a collapsed container renders.
close_reason: Tree-level contract implemented in PR #42 (NodeFacts/ContainerFacts, present() offers+collapses containers, submit inherits valueShape). Deferred follow-ups filed: 8o0 (PR B: extraction + §3 scalar-choice relocation + node.validation removal), and the §7 collapsed-control rendering bead.

title: Close the ADR 029 dual period: parser emits facts-only, present() sole widget/parts source
description:
Follow-up to the wsr tracer (ADR 029). wsr landed the ADDITIVE seam (FieldFacts on nodes, Core present() + defaultPresentation + input/select/multiselect derivers, useSchemaForm wired through present(), conformance folded over the presented tree, golden e2e). The dual period is intentionally still open: the JSON Schema parser STILL assigns widget + builds parts in createFieldNode/arrayNode, and present(defaultPresentation) reproduces those choices identity-preservingly. So widget/parts derivation is duplicated in two places that must agree (guarded by conformance + present unit tests).

This bead closes the dual period: make the parser emit facts-only field nodes and let present() be the SOLE source of widget + parts.

Why it is its own PR (blast radius): FieldNode currently REQUIRES widget+parts. Removing parser assignment means either (a) widget/parts become non-authoritative-until-present, or (b) jsonSchemaToTree itself runs present(defaultPresentation) internally after building facts-only intermediates. Every direct `jsonSchemaToTree(schema)` -> render path (the @jsonschema-form/vanilla package, conformance, arrays/renderer/render-stability tests, ~15 files) must go through a presented tree. Doing this in the same PR that introduces present() would remove the fallback while present() is still unproven; the ADR explicitly sanctions a dual period, so we keep it one PR longer and collapse the duplication here with present() already battle-tested on main.

Scope: parser builds facts-only leaves; jsonSchemaToTree applies defaultPresentation so its return stays fully-formed for direct renders; delete buildInputAttrs/select-parts duplication from fieldNode.ts + createMultiselectFieldNode parts in arrayNode.ts; migrate redundant parser unit tests (which assert widget/parts) into present() unit tests or integration tests. Gate green throughout.

Still deferred beyond this: field.control engine/adapter rewrite; bindRenders custom-widget DX; origin rename (schema -> origin {source, schema: TOrigin}).

title: ValidationSummary should respect the showErrorsWhen display policy (ADR 027)
description: After ADR 027 flipped the default display policy to 'touched', per-field errors are gated by touched/submitted, but ValidationSummary (packages/react/src/ValidationSummary.tsx) reads useValidationIssues() directly and lists every issue that EXISTS, ungated. With live validation + default 'touched' + a mounted summary, the summary leaks all errors immediately while fields stay quiet — inconsistent UX. Decide: gate the summary on submit/touched too (probably show summary only after submit, matching its 'submit-time error summary' docstring), or make it read a policy-aware slice. Not urgent: current demos don't regress (App_09 has no live issues pre-submit; App_11 uses 'always').

title: Field-scoped (subtree) revalidation to avoid whole-document validator cost per event
description: DIRECTION (approved 2026-07-01): measure-first. ADR 023 already made re-renders O(changed-fields), so the only remaining per-event cost is the whole-document Validator (assemble + clone + AJV validate, all O(document)). Before designing any field-scoped/subtree validation (which is a big change and breaks cross-field rules), BENCHMARK the real per-event validator cost at increasing form sizes. The benchmark number is the gate: only build field-scoping if whole-document validation is genuinely hot at realistic sizes; otherwise defer with evidence (h05 collapses to at most a debounce hook + scoped issue-commit). Benchmark lives in packages/validation-ajv/bench/.

title: Native form-state: touched tracking + showErrorsWhen display policy
description: Don't show a field's error until touched/blur (RHF-style). Agnostic policy fn (issues+touched+mode -> shown paths) on the ADR 023 store + per-framework binding. Build AFTER the RHF spike reveals the seam. Boundary: native owns values+issues+touched+policy; dirty/reset/watch/async => form-lib adapter.

title: Spike: React Hook Form as the form-state layer (example/recipe, ADR 024)
description: Wire RHF over our Core tree + renderer; our Validator (ajv) as RHF resolver; touched-gated error display (mode:onTouched). Goal: prove Validator-seam reuse, discover the form-state/display seam shape, test renderer-seam expressiveness (register() injection). Example in examples/, not a package.
close_reason: Merged as PR #12 — recipe lives in examples/basic-react/src/App_12_React+ReactHookForm.tsx. Findings drove ADR 025 (impl: jsonschema-form-ml0) and notes on r1p (Standard Schema) and 8k8 (path-utils consolidation).

title: Presentation stage over neutral facts (ADR 029)
description: EPIC. Superseded framing from ADR 022. Per ADR 029: the parser produces neutral FieldFacts (label, primitive, valueShape, choices, constraints, attrs, origin) and NO widget; a dedicated pure present() stage in @jsonschema-form/core assigns widget + derives a control facet, driven by a layered PresentationResolver (shipped defaultPresentation under an optional source-agnostic consumer resolver). Core owns the built-in widget catalog (neutral derivers, shared with the string oracle for conformance); adapters bindRenders to catalog names and may extend with typed custom widgets. One unified field.control slot (no raw-vs-archetype tiers). Closed Core archetype union for typing; custom widgets branded + typed args only within the const catalog; args (not options) is the generic per-widget config bag.
